questions.question: Define Klein 4-Group and list its elements.
keyPoints: Definition: The Klein 4-Group (V4 or K4) is a finite abelian group of order 4
Elements: V4 = {e, a, b, c} where e is identity
Each non-identity element has order 2: a² = b² = c² = e

questions.question: Show that Klein 4-Group is abelian but not cyclic.
keyPoints: Abelian: For all x,y ∈ V4, x*y = y*x (Cayley table is symmetric)
Not cyclic: No single element generates all 4 elements
Each non-identity element has order 2, not 4
Therefore cannot be cyclic since cyclic group of order 4 requires element of order 4

questions.question: Construct the Cayley table for V4 and verify that it forms a group.
keyPoints: Table rows/columns: e, a, b, c
Identity: e on diagonal and row/column headers
Closure: All entries in table are from V4
Associativity: By property of direct product Z2×Z2
Each element has inverse: a*a=e, b*b=e, c*c=e, e*e=e

questions.question: Find all subgroups of Klein 4-Group.
keyPoints: Five subgroups: {e}, {e,a}, {e,b}, {e,c}, V4
Each non-trivial subgroup has order 2 (by Lagrange's theorem)
All subgroups are normal (since V4 is abelian)
Each subgroup is cyclic with generator of order 2

questions.question: Show that V4 is isomorphic to Z2 × Z2.
keyPoints: Define mapping φ: V4 → Z2 × Z2
Verify φ preserves operation: φ(xy) = φ(x) + φ(y)
Mapping is bijective (one-to-one and onto)
Therefore V4 ≅ Z2 × Z2

questions.question: Give a real-world example of Klein 4-Group.
keyPoints: Symmetry group of a rectangle (excluding 90° rotations)
Four symmetries: Identity (e), vertical flip (a), horizontal flip (b), 180° rotation (c)
These four operations form V4 under composition
Also applies to rhombus (diamond shape) symmetries

questions.question: Prove that in V4, the product of any two distinct non-identity elements equals the third non-identity element.
keyPoints: Let V4 = {e, a, b, c} with a²=b²=c²=e
Consider ab: Since ab ≠ a and ab ≠ b (otherwise b=e or a=e)
Thus ab must equal c (the only remaining element)
Similarly, bc = a and ca = b
This property defines V4's structure completely

questions.question: Prove that Klein 4-Group (V4) is isomorphic to Z2 × Z2. Show detailed verification of group homomorphism properties.
keyPoints: One-to-one: Different elements map to different pairs
Onto: Every element in Z2 × Z2 has preimage
Operation preservation: Verify using Cayley table that φ(xy) = φ(x) + φ(y) for all x,y
Since φ is bijective homomorphism, V4 ≅ Z2 × Z2

questions.question: Construct the complete Cayley table for Klein 4-Group and hence prove that: (i) it forms an abelian group (ii) every non-identity element has order 2 (iii) it is not cyclic.
keyPoints: Draw 4×4 table with e,a,b,c as rows and columns
Fill entries: e*anything = anything, x*x = e for all x, x*y = z for distinct x,y,z
Proof of abelian: Table is symmetric about main diagonal
Proof of order 2: For x∈{a,b,c}, x²=e, so order is 2
Proof not cyclic: No element has order 4 to generate all four elements
Only possible cyclic group of order 4 is Z4, which V4 is not isomorphic to

questions.question: Find all subgroups of Klein 4-Group and verify Lagrange's Theorem. Also prove that all subgroups are normal.
keyPoints: Subgroups: H1={e}, H2={e,a}, H3={e,b}, H4={e,c}, H5=V4
Lagrange verification: Orders divide 4: |H1|=1|4, |H2|=2|4, |H3|=2|4, |H4|=2|4, |H5|=4|4
Normality proof: In abelian groups, all subgroups are normal
For any H ≤ V4 and any g ∈ V4, gH = Hg (verified by commutativity)
Each subgroup of order 2 has index 2, hence normal

questions.question: Show that the set of symmetries of a rectangle (excluding 90° rotations) forms a Klein 4-Group under composition of symmetries.
keyPoints: Identify four symmetries: identity (e), vertical reflection (a), horizontal reflection (b), 180° rotation (c)
Verify closure: composition of any two symmetries gives another in the set
Find identity: doing nothing (e)
Find inverses: each symmetry is its own inverse (order 2)
Verify associativity: composition of symmetries is associative
Check abelian: a∘b = b∘a = c (reflections commute for rectangle)
Thus forms group isomorphic to V4
